title: Marbella Port Speedboat Rental 2026: Costs, Licences and Top Boats
html:
Hiring a speedboat from Marbella Port offers a fast, flexible way to explore the Costa del Sol coastline, with prices starting at around €450 for a half-day self-drive charter. BoatHire24 connects you with verified owners so you can compare options and book securely for your 2026 trip.

Where to set off in Marbella Port

The main departure point is Puerto Deportivo de Marbella, a modern marina with direct access to the Mediterranean. From here you can head east towards the dunes of Artola or west to the exclusive Puerto Banús. Real anchorages include Cabopino Marina, a quieter spot with a sandy beach, and the sheltered waters off the coast of Benalmádena. For a longer run, the island of Isla de las Palomas (Torre del Mar) lies about 20 nautical miles east.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Marbella Port?

Half-day self-drive speedboat rentals typically cost between €400 and €700, while full-day charters start around €800. Prices depend on boat size, engine power and season. For a precise match to your budget, browse boats for your dates on BoatHire24.

Which boat to rent

  1. Axopar 28, best for speed and style, seats up to 8 guests, from €550 for half-day. For direct booking details, see the Limpopo for hire.
  2. Rib tender, ideal for two to four people wanting a nimble run to a beach club, from €300.
  3. Open bowrider (e.g. Four Winns), versatile family choice, 6 guests, from €450.
  4. Day cruiser with cabin, comfortable for 6, 8, includes basic galley, from €700.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Marbella Port?

If you hold a recognised powerboat licence or ICC, you can self-drive up to 24 metres; otherwise you must hire a skipper. Spanish law requires the original licence or a certified translation. The operator will confirm exact requirements at booking. A useful tip: many Marbella boat owners offer a discounted rate if you take their onboard skipper, who also knows the local anchorages.

For the best sea conditions, plan your trip between May and October; the Levante wind can build in the afternoon, so morning departures are often calmer around Cabopino Bay.

How booking works on BoatHire24

After finding a boat you like, you send a booking request with your preferred date and duration. The owner confirms availability within 24 hours and payment is handled securely through the platform. For the cancellation policy and other details, please refer to the booking FAQ.

At a glance

  • Price range: €300, €1,200
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Typical trip length: half-day (4 hours) or full-day (8 hours)
  • Guest capacity: 2, 8 passengers
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Marbella Port

Can I rent a speedboat without a licence in Marbella Port?

Yes, you can charter a boat with a licensed skipper, which is often the simplest option for groups without prior experience.

What is the best time of day to cruise from Marbella Port?

Morning departures (around 10:00) give you calm seas and the best light for photography before midday heat.

Are there any hidden costs when renting a speedboat at Marbella Port?

Fuel is usually charged extra based on usage, and some operators add a cleaning fee of €30, €50 for returning the boat dirty.

Can I anchor and swim off the boat?

Yes, most rentals allow swimming stops in designated areas such as the coves near Punta Calaburra, as long as you keep a safe distance from other vessels.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Marbella Port or list your own. To see current availability and compare prices, check availability on the platform. If you own a boat in Marbella, you can list it on BoatHire24 and begin earning rental income.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marbella Port and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
